codebase-aware-branding.description: Branding skill adapts its interview questions and output based on detected project type (webapp, CLI, library, etc.)

visual-brand-guidelines.signature: BRANDING.md with color palette, typography, spacing, component style, interaction patterns (webapp) or output formatting, error style (CLI)
visual-brand-guidelines.description: Output covers visual brand identity appropriate to the project's interface type

no-webapp-assumptions.description: Non-webapp projects (CLI tools, libraries) receive appropriate brand questions -- never asked about color palettes or component styles

codebase-detection.description: Project type auto-detected from codebase analysis (package.json, Cargo.toml, presence of frontend/ directory, etc.)

definition.scope: Refocuses the branding skill from documentation tone to visual UI/UX brand guidelines, with codebase awareness to adapt questions and output based on project type (webapp vs CLI vs library).

tasks.description: Add codebase detection to identify project type (webapp, CLI, library, etc.)
tasks.acceptance: Branding skill correctly identifies project type from codebase analysis

tasks.description: Restructure interview questions for visual UI/UX focus
tasks.acceptance: Webapp projects asked about color palette, typography, component style; CLI projects asked about output formatting

tasks.description: Update BRANDING.md output format with visual brand sections
tasks.acceptance: Generated BRANDING.md includes project-type-appropriate visual identity guidelines

acceptance: Branding skill detects project type and adapts accordingly
Webapp projects get visual brand guidelines (colors, typography, components)
Non-webapp projects get appropriate brand guidelines (CLI formatting, error style)
BRANDING.md output is actionable for development, not just documentation tone
